alerts.title: Iran nuclear deal hits impasse over enriched uranium stockpile transfer
alerts.summary: Reuters cites Iranian sources that Supreme Leader Khamenei has decreed enriched uranium must stay in Iran, while ynetnews reports Trump assured Israel there will be no deal without dismantling the program. Iran has publicly denied any agreement to hand over its stockpile (TradingView), with Haaretz framing the nuclear question as the central unresolved obstacle hours before a possible signing. Breakout estimate remains 1-2 weeks at 60% U-235.

alerts.title: Trump says he was 'an hour away' from ordering Iran strike before postponing
alerts.summary: CNBC reports Trump publicly disclosed he was one hour from a strike decision on Iran before holding off, with The Jerusalem Post citing Gulf State diplomatic pressure as the proximate cause of the pause. The revelation underscores how close the theater came to direct US-Iran kinetic action and adds urgency to the still-unfinalized MoU, even as threat level remains at 9/10 (CRITICAL).

alerts.title: Reported shooting near White House, alleged second attempt on Trump
alerts.summary: Rybar (~82K views) reports Secret Service shot a 21-year-old who opened fire at 17th Street and Pennsylvania Avenue while Trump was in residence, identifying the suspect as Nasir Best — previously detained for a July White House perimeter breach. Journalists on the lawn were reportedly evacuated to the briefing room. No Western-source corroboration in the current feed; treat as single-source pending confirmation.

alerts.title: Bahrain sentences nine to life for alleged cooperation with IRGC
alerts.summary: Al Jazeera reports Bahrain has handed down life sentences to nine defendants convicted of cooperating with Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. The ruling lands as Gulf states publicly push for diplomacy on the Trump-Iran MoU and signals that Manama is hedging — preserving a hard counter-IRGC judicial posture even as regional de-escalation language dominates headlines.

alerts.title: US deploying defensive 'umbrella' to shield shipping in Strait of Hormuz
alerts.summary: DefenseScoop reports the US is positioning an integrated defense and tech 'umbrella' around commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, amid widespread GPS jamming reported across the region (The Week). The posture complements USS Florida's continued Persian Gulf deployment and follows a BBC survivor account of a recent Hormuz missile strike, suggesting hostile-environment hardening even as MoU talks advance.

analysis_note: The US-Iran MoU is visibly fraying over the uranium-stockpile question even as Gulf diplomacy holds off a strike Trump now admits was an hour from execution; threat level remains at 9/10 with proxy and Ukraine theaters still actively kinetic.
